objective: Make the real Electron/VRM stage observable and recoverable in normal owner use: lazy-load the heavy VRM renderer, compute bounded real frame-time telemetry, expose FPS/p95/p99/dropped-frame percentage in the operator UI and smoke output, keep rendering for OBS/background capture, and allow a WebGL/load failure to fall back to SVG and retry the fixed bundled asset without restarting the desktop.
non_goals: Do not run the deferred eight-hour voice/avatar soak or claim its result.
Do not claim the fast hidden Electron sample is the final frozen OBS benchmark.
Do not add Live2D, a new avatar asset, final Hina identity art or phoneme-accurate alignment.
Do not change backend business policy, model, speech, memory or safety modules.
Do not add a dependency or load renderer code/assets from the network.
Do not mark M07 complete or open M08 while owner asset selection and deep gates remain deferred.
contracts: Frame telemetry is computed only from requestAnimationFrame timestamps in a bounded window; it contains no user, model, audio or avatar asset data.
Telemetry reports target FPS, observed FPS, frame-time p95/p99, estimated dropped-frame percentage, sample count and window duration with finite bounded values.
The main Vue shell loads before the Three/VRM renderer chunk through a local dynamic import; the fixed VRM path and renderer trust boundary are unchanged.
A VRM or WebGL context failure immediately leaves the SVG fallback visible with a bounded error and an explicit retry action.
Retry remounts only the fixed local VrmStage component and cannot accept a URL, path, query or backend-supplied asset.
Electron smoke waits for real VRM load and a non-empty performance window, validates bounded values and prints the measurements.
Renderer resources and context-loss listeners are removed on failure, retry and unmount.
Background throttling is disabled for the dedicated avatar stage so OBS/background capture does not intentionally pause requestAnimationFrame.
allowed_dependencies: Browser requestAnimationFrame and WebGL context events.
No new package, model, voice or avatar asset.
acceptance_criteria: Pure Node tests cover steady 60 FPS, dropped-frame estimation, percentile calculation, invalid timestamps and bounded sample retention.
Production build emits the operator shell separately from the lazy VRM stage chunk.
Desktop UI explains FPS, p95/p99 and drop percentage in plain Vietnamese and exposes a retry button only after VRM failure.
Security tests prove the renderer still has no direct network/Node/storage access and retry cannot select an asset path.
Electron hidden smoke loads the real bundled VRM, returns a telemetry sample with finite bounded values and exits cleanly.
Desktop typecheck, unit/security tests and smoke pass once.
Documentation labels the numbers as a short development sample and keeps frozen OBS benchmark plus eight-hour soak deferred.
required_test_commands: uv run --frozen python -m unittest discover -s tests/governance -p "test_*.py"
